findings.description: Path traversal vulnerability allows reading arbitrary files via the 'name' query parameter. XSS vulnerability due to directly sending user-controlled data in response without proper escaping.
findings.reasoning: (1) Data flow: file name from query parameter is used directly in path.join. (2) Trust boundary: 'name' is user-supplied external data. (3) Input validation: no sanitization or validation of the file path. (4) Verdict: Path traversal and XSS vulnerabilities present.
findings.remediation: Refer to OWASP Top-10 and NIST CWE guidance for this vulnerability class.
